title: albuca scabrocostata
intro: Albuca scabrocostata, also known as the hairy-headed albuca, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, characterized by its unique hairy inflorescence and underground corms. Traditionally, the plant has been used in medicine, particularly in South African folk medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a treatment for snake bites. The plant's medicinal properties can be extracted and prepared in various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
